sourceRefs.fallbackText: Eli Bendersky, on behalf of the Go team 8 August 2023
sourceRefs.textQuote: Eli Bendersky, on behalf of the Go team 8 August 2023

highSignalBlocks.text: Eli Bendersky, on behalf of the Go team 8 August 2023

sourceRefs.fallbackText: Today the Go team is thrilled to release Go 1.21, which you can get by visiting the download page .
sourceRefs.textQuote: Today the Go team is thrilled to release Go 1.21, which you can get by visiting the download page .

highSignalBlocks.text: Today the Go team is thrilled to release Go 1.21, which you can get by visiting the download page .

sourceRefs.fallbackText: Go 1.21 is packed with new features and improvements. Here are some of the notable changes; for the full list, refer to the release notes .
sourceRefs.textQuote: Go 1.21 is packed with new features and improvements. Here are some of the notable changes; for the full list, refer to the release notes .

highSignalBlocks.text: Go 1.21 is packed with new features and improvements. Here are some of the notable changes; for the full list, refer to the release notes .

sourceRefs.fallbackText: The Profile Guided Optimization (PGO) feature we announced for preview in 1.20 is now generally available! If a file named default.pgo is present in the main package’s directory, the go command will use it to enable a PGO build. See the PGO documentation for more details. We’ve measured the impact of PGO on a wide set of Go programs and see performance impro
sourceRefs.textQuote: The Profile Guided Optimization (PGO) feature we announced for preview in 1.20 is now generally available! If a file named default.pgo is present in the main package’s directory, the go command will use it to enable a PG

highSignalBlocks.text: The Profile Guided Optimization (PGO) feature we announced for preview in 1.20 is now generally available! If a file named default.pgo is present in the main package’s directory, the go command will use it to enable a PGO build. See the PGO documentation for more details. We’ve measured the impact of PGO on a wide set of Go programs and see performance improvements of 2-7%.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: The go tool now supports backward and forward language compatibility.
sourceRefs.textQuote: The go tool now supports backward and forward language compatibility.

highSignalBlocks.text: The go tool now supports backward and forward language compatibility.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: New built-in functions: min, max and clear .
sourceRefs.textQuote: New built-in functions: min, max and clear .

highSignalBlocks.text: New built-in functions: min, max and clear .

sourceRefs.fallbackText: Several improvements to type inference for generic functions. The description of type inference in the spec has been expanded and clarified.
sourceRefs.textQuote: Several improvements to type inference for generic functions. The description of type inference in the spec has been expanded and clarified.

highSignalBlocks.text: Several improvements to type inference for generic functions. The description of type inference in the spec has been expanded and clarified.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: In a future version of Go we’re planning to address one of the most common gotchas of Go programming: loop variable capture . Go 1.21 comes with a preview of this feature that you can enable in your code using an environment variable. See the LoopvarExperiment wiki page for more details.
sourceRefs.textQuote: In a future version of Go we’re planning to address one of the most common gotchas of Go programming: loop variable capture . Go 1.21 comes with a preview of this feature that you can enable in your code using an environ

highSignalBlocks.text: In a future version of Go we’re planning to address one of the most common gotchas of Go programming: loop variable capture . Go 1.21 comes with a preview of this feature that you can enable in your code using an environment variable. See the LoopvarExperiment wiki page for more details.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: New log/slog package for structured logging.
sourceRefs.textQuote: New log/slog package for structured logging.

highSignalBlocks.text: New log/slog package for structured logging.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: New slices package for common operations on slices of any element type. This includes sorting functions that are generally faster and more ergonomic than the sort package.
sourceRefs.textQuote: New slices package for common operations on slices of any element type. This includes sorting functions that are generally faster and more ergonomic than the sort package.

highSignalBlocks.text: New slices package for common operations on slices of any element type. This includes sorting functions that are generally faster and more ergonomic than the sort package.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: New maps package for common operations on maps of any key or element type.
sourceRefs.textQuote: New maps package for common operations on maps of any key or element type.

highSignalBlocks.text: New maps package for common operations on maps of any key or element type.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: New cmp package with new utilities for comparing ordered values.
sourceRefs.textQuote: New cmp package with new utilities for comparing ordered values.

highSignalBlocks.text: New cmp package with new utilities for comparing ordered values.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: In addition to the performance improvements when enabling PGO:
sourceRefs.textQuote: In addition to the performance improvements when enabling PGO:

highSignalBlocks.text: In addition to the performance improvements when enabling PGO:

sourceRefs.fallbackText: The Go compiler itself has been rebuilt with PGO enabled for 1.21, and as a result it builds Go programs 2-4% faster, depending on the host architecture.
sourceRefs.textQuote: The Go compiler itself has been rebuilt with PGO enabled for 1.21, and as a result it builds Go programs 2-4% faster, depending on the host architecture.

highSignalBlocks.text: The Go compiler itself has been rebuilt with PGO enabled for 1.21, and as a result it builds Go programs 2-4% faster, depending on the host architecture.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: Due to tuning of the garbage collector, some applications may see up to a 40% reduction in tail latency.
sourceRefs.textQuote: Due to tuning of the garbage collector, some applications may see up to a 40% reduction in tail latency.

highSignalBlocks.text: Due to tuning of the garbage collector, some applications may see up to a 40% reduction in tail latency.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: Collecting traces with runtime/trace now incurs a substantially smaller CPU cost on amd64 and arm64.
sourceRefs.textQuote: Collecting traces with runtime/trace now incurs a substantially smaller CPU cost on amd64 and arm64.

highSignalBlocks.text: Collecting traces with runtime/trace now incurs a substantially smaller CPU cost on amd64 and arm64.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: Go 1.21 adds an experimental port for WebAssembly System Interface (WASI) , Preview 1 ( GOOS=wasip1 , GOARCH=wasm ).
sourceRefs.textQuote: Go 1.21 adds an experimental port for WebAssembly System Interface (WASI) , Preview 1 ( GOOS=wasip1 , GOARCH=wasm ).

highSignalBlocks.text: Go 1.21 adds an experimental port for WebAssembly System Interface (WASI) , Preview 1 ( GOOS=wasip1 , GOARCH=wasm ).

sourceRefs.fallbackText: To facilitate writing more general WebAssembly (Wasm) code, the compiler also supports a new directive for importing functions from the Wasm host: go:wasmimport .
sourceRefs.textQuote: To facilitate writing more general WebAssembly (Wasm) code, the compiler also supports a new directive for importing functions from the Wasm host: go:wasmimport .

highSignalBlocks.text: To facilitate writing more general WebAssembly (Wasm) code, the compiler also supports a new directive for importing functions from the Wasm host: go:wasmimport .

sourceRefs.fallbackText: Thanks to everyone who contributed to this release by writing code, filing bugs, sharing feedback, and testing the release candidates. Your efforts helped to ensure that Go 1.21 is as stable as possible. As always, if you notice any problems, please file an issue .
sourceRefs.textQuote: Thanks to everyone who contributed to this release by writing code, filing bugs, sharing feedback, and testing the release candidates. Your efforts helped to ensure that Go 1.21 is as stable as possible. As always, if yo

highSignalBlocks.text: Thanks to everyone who contributed to this release by writing code, filing bugs, sharing feedback, and testing the release candidates. Your efforts helped to ensure that Go 1.21 is as stable as possible. As always, if you notice any problems, please file an issue .
